diagrams.description: This timeline traces the evolution of Clinical Psychology. It begins with early humanitarian reforms (Pinel unchains patients in 1793) and the birth of the term in 1896 when Lightner Witmer founded the first psychological clinic. Freud’s psychoanalysis (1900) and Binet’s intelligence test (1905) provided foundational tools. The Boulder Conference (1949) established the scientist‑practitioner model. Major developments: Ellis’s REBT (1955) and Beck’s cognitive therapy (1960s‑70s), the rise of behavior therapy. The DSM‑III (1980) revolutionized diagnosis, and APA’s first clinical guidelines appeared in 1995. Recent decades saw the emergence of positive psychology (2000), the evidence‑based practice movement (2005), teletherapy expansion (2010s), and the integration of digital mental health tools (2020s).
